text:
Once upon a time, in a little village nestled among pine woods, there lived a wise old owl named Olga. She had the richest white feather plumage you'd ever seen and slept atop Oakredeough.

One rainy afternoon, a clever young raccoon named Rupert decided to visit Olivia Oakden.

"Hellocks!" he said apologetically. "What boring old owl you must be! Owls don't talk much."

Olga just hooted softly.

"I hear marvelous things about you!" Rupert went on. "Like... what? Owls with fancy hats?"

"A hawk once wore mine."

"A hawk? Like that owl? I wish."

"The ring wears him loose."

"Oh dear.”

"Anyway," said Rupert earnestly," I'd like to hear your stories. You must know fascinating things."

"Only things my big head overlooks."

"But you think about them!"

"Sometimes."

One rainy evening, they talked.

*Rupert listened attentively as Olga recounted tales
